# CodeBolt Documentation

> Product documentation for CodeBolt, an AI-native coding environment with multi-agent orchestration, MCP tooling, and extensible agent architecture.

This file provides a curated index of the most important documentation. Each link points to a clean markdown mirror suitable for LLM retrieval. For exhaustive API/type details see the Reference section overviews.

## Start Here

- [Index](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/index.md)

## Using Codebolt

- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/overview.md): Reference documentation for every feature. Jump to what you need — this isn't meant to be read cover-to-cover.
- [What is Codebolt](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/what-is-codebolt.md): Codebolt is an AI-native coding environment
- [Quickstart](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/quickstart.md): Want to use OpenAI, Anthropic, or a local model? See Installation & Setup.
- [Clients Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/overview.md): Codebolt is the same agent runtime no matter how you talk to it
- [Quickstart](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/desktop-app/quickstart.md): Get the desktop app running and complete your first agent task.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/cli/quickstart.md): If you have the desktop app installed you already have the CLI — skip to step 3.
- [CLI Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/cli/overview.md): The codebolt CLI is the command-line entry point for running the Codebolt server, launching the TUI, connecting to an existing server, running headless prompts,.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/tui/quickstart.md): The TUI requires the CLI to be installed. If you haven't installed it yet, see CLI Quickstart first — the sign-in and provider setup there applies here too.
- [TUI Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/surfaces/tui/overview.md): Codebolt's terminal UI is powered by @codebolt/gotui
- [Chat Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/chat/overview.md): The chat panel is the primary way most people interact with Codebolt. You pick an agent, type a message, watch it work, review the result
- [Installation & Setup](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/installation.md): Get Codebolt installed and configured in a few minutes. Pick your interface below — each tab walks through install, sign-in, and first-run setup end to end.
- [Code & Terminal Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/code-and-terminal/overview.md): The Code and Terminal panels are the primary workspace for editing files, running commands, and reviewing changes
- [What is an Agent](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/agents/what-is-an-agent.md): From a user's point of view, an agent is the "who" that handles your chat
- [Agent Extensions Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/agent-extensions/overview.md): Agent Extensions are modular units of functionality that augment what agents can do
- [Agent Tools Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/tools-and-mcp/overview.md): Agent Tools are the built-in capabilities available to every agent — the panel-based interfaces and programmatic tool families that agents use to interact with.
- [Agent Observability Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/agent-observability/overview.md): Observability in Codebolt is how you see what an agent did, what it's doing right now, and why it made a particular choice
- [Eval & Optimization Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/eval-and-optimization/overview.md): Codebolt's Eval & Optimization system lets you scientifically measure agent quality and automatically improve it
- [Planning](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/planning/overview.md): Codebolt includes a set of built-in planning tools that live alongside your code and your agents
- [Memory & Context Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/memory/overview.md): Memory is how Codebolt agents accumulate, retain, and retrieve knowledge — across turns, across sessions, and across projects
- [Multi-Agent Usage Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/multi-agent-usage/overview.md): This section covers running multi-agent work — swarms, flows, review/merge — as a user
- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/parallel-agents/overview.md): Codebolt lets you run multiple agents at the same time — on the same project, on different tasks, or coordinated in a pipeline
- [Coordination Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/agent-coordination/overview.md): When multiple agents work on the same project, they need ways to divide work, communicate, reach decisions, and stay aware of what others are doing
- [Stigmergic Coordination Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/stigmergic-coordination/overview.md): When several agents work on the same project — jobs, review requests, file edits — they need a way to stay out of each other's way and signal intent to each.
- [What are Plugins](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/plugins/overview.md): Plugins extend the Codebolt application itself — not just what agents can do, but what the application can do. They run as separate Node.js processes alongside the server and connect via WebSocket, so they can add entirely new capabilities without modifying Codebolt's core.
- [Environments Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/environments/overview.md): An Environment in Codebolt defines the execution context for agent runs — the shell, environment variables, runtime versions, and crucially, where the agent.
- [Automation Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/auto-interactivity/overview.md): Automation covers everything that causes agents to act automatically — without a user typing a message
- [Custom Agentic Application Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/08da-custom-agentic-application/overview.md): Custom agentic applications are product-specific applications built on top of Codebolt.
- [Autonomous Companies Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/08db-autonomous-companies/overview.md): Use Codebolt to model autonomous company workflows with virtual employees, shared goals, communication channels, monitoring triggers, and coordination rules.
- [Self Hosted Agent Infrastructure Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/08dc-self-hosted-custom-agents/overview.md): Use Codebolt as self-hosted agent infrastructure across local machines, company environments, sandboxes, cloud runtimes, and external channels.
- [External Integrations Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/external-integrations/overview.md): External Integrations connect your agents to the outside world — chat platforms like Telegram and Slack, project management tools like Linear and GitHub, and.
- [Cloud Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/cloud/overview.md): Everything you do in the desktop app — run agents, edit code, install from the marketplace, manage providers — you can also do from a browser against.
- [What are Plugins](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/plugins/overview.md): Plugins extend the Codebolt application itself — not just what agents can do, but what the application can do. They run as separate Node
- [Code Observability Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/using-codebolt/code-observability/overview.md): Codebolt includes code-observability tools for understanding both how a project is wired together and how an agent execution moved through that code before you.

## Concepts

- [Concepts Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/concepts/overview.md): The what and why of Codebolt — the mental models you need to understand the rest of the docs.
- [Architecture](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/concepts/foundation/architecture.md): Codebolt is organized as five cooperating planes, each with a clear responsibility

## Guides

- [Guides Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/guides/overview.md): Task-oriented walkthroughs. Each guide answers a specific "how do I do X?" with numbered steps and a concrete outcome

## Build on Codebolt

- [Build on Codebolt](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/overview.md): This section is for developers extending Codebolt. If you want to use Codebolt, start at Using Codebolt. If you want to follow a specific how-to, try Guides
- [Architecture for Builders](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/architecture-for-builders.md): If you are building on Codebolt, start with this mental model:
- [Creating Agents](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/creating-agents/overview.md): This subsection is about authoring agents. If you want an AI specialised for your codebase, your domain, your coding conventions — this is where you build it.
- [Custom Agent Quickstart](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/creating-agents/quickstart.md): Build and run a minimal custom agent in ~15 minutes. Covers level 0 (remix), level 1 (framework), and remote agents
- [Patterns Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/creating-agents/patterns/overview.md): The @codebolt/agent package provides two exports:
- [Agent Extensions Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/overview.md): Agent extensions are the shared primitives that any Codebolt agent can use without the agent author having to build them
- [Capabilities](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/capabilities/overview.md): A capability is a versioned, self-contained unit of agent functionality in Codebolt's unified extensibility framework
- [Skills](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/skills/overview.md): A skill is a named, reusable competency an agent can call for a narrow task. A tool does one mechanical thing. A skill does one cognitive thing.
- [MCP Tools Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/mcp-tools/overview.md): How to add MCP tools to a Codebolt app and how to build new MCP servers for agents.
- [Quickstart — Local MCP Server](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/mcp-tools/quickstart-local-mcp.md): Create a local MCP tool, register it with Codebolt, and have an agent call it. This covers two approaches:
- [Action Blocks](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-extensions/action-blocks/overview.md): An action block is a reusable, self-contained TypeScript module that an agent can invoke by name
- [Custom Interfaces Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/custom-uis/overview.md): Build your own interface on top of Codebolt: a chat panel, a CLI, an IDE extension, a web dashboard, a mobile companion, or a product-specific front-end.
- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/custom-agentic-applications/overview.md): Custom agentic applications are product-specific applications built on top of the Codebolt server
- [Plugins Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/plugins/overview.md): Plugins extend the CodeBolt application. They run as separate Node
- [Chat Gateway Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/plugins/chat-gateway/overview.md): The Chat Gateway is the server-side routing layer behind Codebolt's channel plugins
- [Custom AI Providers](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/plugins/custom-ai-providers/overview.md): Custom AI providers let plugins register AI backends that appear as selectable providers inside Codebolt.
- [Proxy Execution Gateway](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/plugins/proxy-execution-gateway/overview.md): The Proxy Execution Gateway is the plugin-side deferred execution mechanism in Codebolt
- [Extending Codebolt](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/extending-codebolt/overview.md): This section is about adding new capabilities to the Codebolt application itself — not to individual agents, but to the platform every agent runs on.
- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/eval-and-optimization/overview.md): Evaluate how well your agents, skills, and action blocks perform on specific tasks — then automatically optimize them using an agent-driven improvement loop.
- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/memory-management/overview.md): Codebolt exposes the full memory stack as a programmable API surface
- [Multi-Agent Orchestration](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/multi-agent-orchestration/overview.md): Designing multi-agent coordination systems in Codebolt
- [Multi-Environment Orchestration](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/multi-environment-orchestration/overview.md): How does Codebolt start, connect, and coordinate agent work when the agent loop runs in another environment?
- [Remote Environments](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/multi-environment-orchestration/remote-environments/overview.md): In Codebolt, a remote environment is a server-managed execution target.
- [Architecture Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/internals/architecture-overview.md): Codebolt is built as a set of cooperating subsystems that run inside packages/server (the long-lived process), with packages/electron + packages/ui providing.
- [Building Integrations](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/building-integrations/overview.md): Patterns for connecting Codebolt to external services and platforms. Each pattern covers: which mechanism to use, how it's wired, and a minimal working example.
- [Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/building-integrations/chat-integrations/overview.md): Connect any chat platform — Discord, Slack, Telegram, or your own — to Codebolt
- [Self-Hosting Overview](https://docs.codebolt.ai/llms/build-on-codebolt/agent-infrastructure/overview.md): Running the Codebolt server yourself instead of as an embedded local process
